en: I have no idea what he's talking about.
ko: 그가 무슨 말을 하는지 전혀 모르겠어요.

en: Play it as cool as you like, Fonzie,
but we all know you've been following my client day and night.
ko: 하지만 우리는 당신들이 내 의뢰인을 낮과 밤으로 따라다녔다는 걸 알고 있어요.

en: The poor man can't even spend a few minutes with his granddaughter
ko: 불쌍한 남자는 손녀와 몇 분도 함께 보낼 수 없어요

en: without you guys quivering in the bushes
and peeping through your little binoculars.
It's... well, it's disturbing.
And it's taken a toll on his mental and physical well-being.
ko: 그리고 그의 정신적, 육체적 건강에 영향을 미쳤어요.

en: The client looks fine to me.
Well, some hurts only show on the inside.
Now, you guys don't even have warrants for these tales, do you?
ko: 이제, 당신들은 이런 이야기들에 대한 영장도 없죠?

en: Theoretically, these tales you refer to
would be completely within the bounds of the law.
Your warrant follows somebody through a public place, theoretically.
ko: 당신의 영장은 누군가를 공공장소에서 따라가는 것이죠, 이론적으로.

en: Yeah, that is, uh, theoretically correct.
However, I would counter that
an open-ended, unrestricted surveillance like this
amounts to, uh, stalking,
which is illegal.
Now, I don't know what it is you find so interesting about my clients,
ko: 이제, 당신들이 내 의뢰인에게 그렇게 흥미를 느끼는 이유가 뭔지 모르겠고,

en: and I'm not here to judge.
